summary: Verified the /admin bug fix across 3 scenarios. Scenarios A and C pass; Scenario B FAILS — the described fix (auto-logout non-admin and render AdminLogin) is NOT implemented in Admin.jsx. Code at line 61 still does `toast.error('Admin access required'); navigate('/')` for non-admin users.
passed: Scenario A: Fresh browser visiting /admin renders [data-testid=admin-login-page] (no 'Admin access required' shown).

Scenario C: Submitting admin-login-email=hakkiveda@gmail.com / admin-login-password=Hakki@Admin2026 logs in successfully and renders [data-testid=admin-dashboard] (lands on Profile tab as expected for must_change_password).

failed.issue: When a customer is logged in and visits /admin, the page does NOT auto-logout and does NOT render AdminLogin. Instead it shows toast 'Admin access required' and redirects to '/'. Customer JWT (hk_token) is still present in localStorage after visiting /admin.
failed.evidence: Registered new customer via /auth (testid auth-email/auth-password/auth-submit, switched via switch-register), landed on /account with token. Visited /admin -> ended on url='/', toast text='Admin access required', admin-login-page visible=false, admin-dashboard=false, hk_token still present in localStorage (no logout occurred). Confirmed by source: /app/frontend/src/pages/Admin.jsx line 61: `if (user && !user.is_admin) { toast.error("Admin access required"); navigate("/"); return; }` — no logout() call, no AdminLogin render path for authenticated non-admin.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Admin.jsx, replace the non-admin branch in the useEffect so that when `user && !user.is_admin`, call `logout()` (do not navigate away). Also change the render guard from `if (!user) return <AdminLogin/>;` to `if (!user || !user.is_admin) return <AdminLogin/>;` so the AdminLogin form is shown for any non-admin (including stale customer JWT).
Optionally drop the `toast.error('Admin access required')` for this case since the user simply needs the admin login form, not an error redirect.
